text_original: Motse© said; The policy of the magnanimous will pursue what procures benefits of the world and destroy its calamities.
text_normalized: Motse© said; The policy of the magnanimous will pursue what procures benefits of the world and destroy its calamities.

text_original: If anything, when established as a law, is beneficial to the people it will be done; if not, it will not be done.
text_normalized: If anything, when established as a law, is beneficial to the people it will be done; if not, it will not be done.

text_original: Moreover, the magnanimous in their care for the world do not think of doing those things which delight the eyes, please the ears, gratify the taste, and ease the body.
text_normalized: Moreover, the magnanimous in their care for the world do not think of doing those things which delight the eyes, please the ears, gratify the taste, and ease the body.

text_original: When these deprive the people of their means of clothing and food, the magnanimous would not undertake them.
text_normalized: When these deprive the people of their means of clothing and food, the magnanimous would not undertake them.

text_original: So the reason why Motse condemns music is not because that the sounds of the big bell, the sounding drum, the ch'in and the she and the yii and the sheng are not pleasant, that the carvings and ornaments are not delightful, that the fried and the broiled meats of the grass-fed and the grain-fed animals are not gratifying, or that the high towers, grand arbours, and quiet villas are not comfortable.
text_normalized: So the reason why Motse condemns music is not because that the sounds of the big bell, the sounding drum, the ch'in and the she and the yii and the sheng are not pleasant, that the carvings and ornaments are not delightful, that the fried and the broiled meats of the grass-fed and the grain-fed animals are not gratifying, or that the high towers, grand arbours, and quiet villas are not comfortable.

text_original: Although the body knows they are comfortable, the mouth knows they are gratifying, the eyes laiow they are delightful, and the ears know they are pleasing, yet they are found not to be in accordance with the deeds of the sage-kings of antiquity and not to contribute to the benefits of the people at present.
text_normalized: Although the body knows they are comfortable, the mouth knows they are gratifying, the eyes laiow they are delightful, and the ears know they are pleasing, yet they are found not to be in accordance with the deeds of the sage-kings of antiquity and not to contribute to the benefits of the people at present.

text_original: And, so, Motse proclaims: To have music is wrong.
text_normalized: And, so, Motse proclaims: To have music is wrong.

text_original: Now the rulers construct musical instruments as an imder-taking of the state.
text_normalized: Now the rulers construct musical instruments as an imder-taking of the state.

text_original: They cannot -be produced as easily as by evaporating water or digging into the earth.
text_normalized: They cannot -be produced as easily as by evaporating water or digging into the earth.

text_original: Inevitably heavy taxes have to be collected from the people to obtain sounds of the big bell, the sounding drum, the ch'in and the she, and the, yli and the sheng.
text_normalized: Inevitably heavy taxes have to be collected from the people to obtain sounds of the big bell, the sounding drum, the ch'in and the she, and the, yli and the sheng.

text_original: The ancient sage-kings had, indeed, collected heavy taxes from the people to build boats and vehicles.
text_normalized: The ancient sage-kings had, indeed, collected heavy taxes from the people to build boats and vehicles.

text_original: But when,, they were completed, and when the people asked: What use have we for these?
text_normalized: But when,, they were completed, and when the people asked: What use have we for these?

text_original: " the answer was: " The boats' are to be employed on water and the vehicles on land, so that the gentlemen can rest their feet and the labourers" can-rest their shoulders and backs.’" Thus the people contributed their money and dared not grumble about it.
text_normalized: " the answer was: " The boats' are to be employed on water and the vehicles on land, so that the gentlemen can rest their feet and the labourers" can-rest their shoulders and backs.’" Thus the people contributed their money and dared not grumble about it.

text_original: This was because the boats and vehicles contributed to the benefit of the people.
text_normalized: This was because the boats and vehicles contributed to the benefit of the people.

text_original: If the musical instruments also contribute to the benefit of the people, even I shall not dare condemn' them.'.
text_normalized: If the musical instruments also contribute to the benefit of the people, even I shall not dare condemn' them.'.

text_original: Thus.'if the musical instruments are as useful as the boats and carts with the' sage-kings, even I shall not dare condemn them.
text_normalized: Thus.'if the musical instruments are as useful as the boats and carts with the' sage-kings, even I shall not dare condemn them.

text_original: There are three things that the people worry about, namely, that the hungry cannot be fed, that the cold cannot be clothed, and that the tired cannot get rest.
text_normalized: There are three things that the people worry about, namely, that the hungry cannot be fed, that the cold cannot be clothed, and that the tired cannot get rest.

text_original: These three are the great worries of the people.
text_normalized: These three are the great worries of the people.

text_original: Now suppose we strike the big bell, boat the sounding drum, play the ch"in and the she, and blow the yix and the sheng, can the material for food and clothing then he procured for the people?
text_normalized: Now suppose we strike the big bell, boat the sounding drum, play the ch"in and the she, and blow the yix and the sheng, can the material for food and clothing then he procured for the people?

text_original: Even I do not think this is possible.
text_normalized: Even I do not think this is possible.

text_original: Again, every large state now attacks small states and every large house molests small houses.
text_normalized: Again, every large state now attacks small states and every large house molests small houses.

text_original: The strong plunder the weak, the many oppress the few the clever deceive the stupid and the honoured disdain the humble.
text_normalized: The strong plunder the weak, the many oppress the few the clever deceive the stupid and the honoured disdain the humble.

text_original: And bandits and.
text_normalized: And bandits and.

text_original: thieves rise aU together and cannot be suppressed.
text_normalized: thieves rise aU together and cannot be suppressed.

text_original: But can the chaos in the; world be put in order by striking the big bell, beating the sounding drum, playing the ch’in and the she, and blowing the yii and the sheng?
text_normalized: But can the chaos in the; world be put in order by striking the big bell, beating the sounding drum, playing the ch’in and the she, and blowing the yii and the sheng?

text_original: Even I do not think it is possible.
text_normalized: Even I do not think it is possible.

text_original: Therefore Motse said: The levy of heavy taxes on the people to construct the big bell, the sounding drum, the ch’in and the she, and the yii and the sheng, is not at all helpful in the endeavour to procure the benefits of the world and destroy its calamities.
text_normalized: Therefore Motse said: The levy of heavy taxes on the people to construct the big bell, the sounding drum, the ch’in and the she, and the yii and the sheng, is not at all helpful in the endeavour to procure the benefits of the world and destroy its calamities.

text_original: Therefore Motse said: To have music is wrong.
text_normalized: Therefore Motse said: To have music is wrong.

text_original: As the rulers look down from a high tower or in a grand arbour, a bell is just like an inverted ting.
text_normalized: As the rulers look down from a high tower or in a grand arbour, a bell is just like an inverted ting.

text_original: If it is not struck wherefrom would come th) pleasure?
text_normalized: If it is not struck wherefrom would come th) pleasure?

text_original: Therefore it ipust be struck.
text_normalized: Therefore it ipust be struck.

text_original: To strike it of course the aged and the yodng would not be employed.
text_normalized: To strike it of course the aged and the yodng would not be employed.

text_original: For their eyes and ears are not keen, their arms are not strong, and they cannot produce an harmonious tone with varied expression.
text_normalized: For their eyes and ears are not keen, their arms are not strong, and they cannot produce an harmonious tone with varied expression.

text_original: So, those in the prime of life must be employed because their eyes and ears are keen, their limbs strong, their voices harmonious and varied in expression.
text_normalized: So, those in the prime of life must be employed because their eyes and ears are keen, their limbs strong, their voices harmonious and varied in expression.

text_original: If men are employed it interferes with their ploughing and planting.
text_normalized: If men are employed it interferes with their ploughing and planting.

text_original: If women are employed it interferes with their weaving and spinning.
text_normalized: If women are employed it interferes with their weaving and spinning.

text_original: Now, the rulers take to music hnd deprive the people of their means of clothing and food to such an extent!
text_normalized: Now, the rulers take to music hnd deprive the people of their means of clothing and food to such an extent!

text_original: Therefore Motse said: To have music is wrong.
text_normalized: Therefore Motse said: To have music is wrong.

text_original: Now when the big bell, the sounding drum, the ch’in and the she, and the yii and the sheng are provided it is yet no pleasure for the lords alone to listen to the playing.
text_normalized: Now when the big bell, the sounding drum, the ch’in and the she, and the yii and the sheng are provided it is yet no pleasure for the lords alone to listen to the playing.

text_original: Therefore they must enjoy it with either the common people or the gentlemen.
text_normalized: Therefore they must enjoy it with either the common people or the gentlemen.

text_original: If with the gentlemen, it will interfere with their attending to government.
text_normalized: If with the gentlemen, it will interfere with their attending to government.

text_original: If with the common people it will interfere with their work.
text_normalized: If with the common people it will interfere with their work.

text_original: Now the rulers take to music and deprive the people of so many of their oppor-tunities to produce food and clothing!
text_normalized: Now the rulers take to music and deprive the people of so many of their oppor-tunities to produce food and clothing!

text_original: Therefore Motse said: It is wrong to have music.
text_normalized: Therefore Motse said: It is wrong to have music.

text_original: Formerly, Lord K’ang of Ch’i (404-379 b.c.) loved music and dancing.
text_normalized: Formerly, Lord K’ang of Ch’i (404-379 b.c.) loved music and dancing.

text_original: The dancers were not to wear garments of coarse cloth or to eat husks and bran.
text_normalized: The dancers were not to wear garments of coarse cloth or to eat husks and bran.

text_original: For if food and drink are not dainty the appearance and complexion will not be enjoyable.
text_normalized: For if food and drink are not dainty the appearance and complexion will not be enjoyable.

text_original: And if clothing is not elegant the body and the movement will not be delightful.
text_normalized: And if clothing is not elegant the body and the movement will not be delightful.

text_original: Therefore their food must consist of grain and meat and their clothing must be made of silk and embroidery.
text_normalized: Therefore their food must consist of grain and meat and their clothing must be made of silk and embroidery.

text_original: They did not produce material of clothing and food at all, but lived on others all the time.
text_normalized: They did not produce material of clothing and food at all, but lived on others all the time.

text_original: Hence Motse said: Now the lords take to music and deprive the people of so many of their opportunities to produce food and clothing!
text_normalized: Hence Motse said: Now the lords take to music and deprive the people of so many of their opportunities to produce food and clothing!

text_original: Therefore Motse proclaimed: To have music is wrong.
text_normalized: Therefore Motse proclaimed: To have music is wrong.

text_original: Also, man is different from birds and beasts and insects.
text_normalized: Also, man is different from birds and beasts and insects.

text_original: The birds, beasts, and insects have their feathers and furs for coats and fur coats, have their hoofs and claws for sandals and shoes, and have water and grass for drink and food.
text_normalized: The birds, beasts, and insects have their feathers and furs for coats and fur coats, have their hoofs and claws for sandals and shoes, and have water and grass for drink and food.

text_original: Therefore the male do not sow seeds or plant trees, neither do the female weave or spin, yet food and clothing are provided.
text_normalized: Therefore the male do not sow seeds or plant trees, neither do the female weave or spin, yet food and clothing are provided.

text_original: Now, man is different from these.
text_normalized: Now, man is different from these.

text_original: Those who exert themselves will live.
text_normalized: Those who exert themselves will live.

text_original: Those who do not exert themselves cannot live.
text_normalized: Those who do not exert themselves cannot live.

text_original: When the gentlemen do not attend to government diligently, the jurisdiction will be in chaos.
text_normalized: When the gentlemen do not attend to government diligently, the jurisdiction will be in chaos.

text_original: When the common men do not attend to work, supply will not be sufficient.
text_normalized: When the common men do not attend to work, supply will not be sufficient.

text_original: If the gentlemen of the world should doubt my word, let us enumerate the several, duties in the world and see the harm music does (to them): For the rulers to go to court early and retire late to listen to lawsuits and attend to government is their duty.
text_normalized: If the gentlemen of the world should doubt my word, let us enumerate the several, duties in the world and see the harm music does (to them): For the rulers to go to court early and retire late to listen to lawsuits and attend to government is their duty.

text_original: For the gentlemen to exhaust the energy of their limbs and employ fully the wisdom of their minds to attend to the court within and to collect taxes without from passes, markets, and products from mountains, woods, and water and fields in order to fill up the granaries and the treasury is their duty.
text_normalized: For the gentlemen to exhaust the energy of their limbs and employ fully the wisdom of their minds to attend to the court within and to collect taxes without from passes, markets, and products from mountains, woods, and water and fields in order to fill up the granaries and the treasury is their duty.

text_original: For the farmers to set out early and come back late, to sow seeds and plant trees in order to produce a large quantity of soy beans and millet is their duty.
text_normalized: For the farmers to set out early and come back late, to sow seeds and plant trees in order to produce a large quantity of soy beans and millet is their duty.

text_original: For the women to rise up at dawn and retire in the night to weave and spin in order to produce much silk, flax linen, and cloth is their duty.
text_normalized: For the women to rise up at dawn and retire in the night to weave and spin in order to produce much silk, flax linen, and cloth is their duty.

text_original: Now if the rulers should love music and listen to it, they would not be able to go to court early and retire late to listen to lawsuits and attend to government.
text_normalized: Now if the rulers should love music and listen to it, they would not be able to go to court early and retire late to listen to lawsuits and attend to government.

text_original: Then the country would be in chaos and the state would be in danger.
text_normalized: Then the country would be in chaos and the state would be in danger.

text_original: If the gentlemen should love music and listen to it, they would not be able to exhaust the energy in their limbs and employ fully the wisdom in the mind to attend to court within and collect taxes without from passes and markets and products from mountains, woods, water, and fields to fill up the granaries and the treasury.
text_normalized: If the gentlemen should love music and listen to it, they would not be able to exhaust the energy in their limbs and employ fully the wisdom in the mind to attend to court within and collect taxes without from passes and markets and products from mountains, woods, water, and fields to fill up the granaries and the treasury.

text_original: Then the granaries and the treasury would not be filled.
text_normalized: Then the granaries and the treasury would not be filled.

text_original: If the farmers should love music and listen to it, they would not be able to set out early and come back late to sow seeds and plant trees and produce a large quantity of soy beans and millet.
text_normalized: If the farmers should love music and listen to it, they would not be able to set out early and come back late to sow seeds and plant trees and produce a large quantity of soy beans and millet.

text_original: Then the soy beans and millet would not be sufficient.
text_normalized: Then the soy beans and millet would not be sufficient.

text_original: If the women sliould love music and listen to it, they would not be able to rise up at dawn and retire in the niglit to weave and spin and produce inncb. silk, flax linen, and cloth.
text_normalized: If the women sliould love music and listen to it, they would not be able to rise up at dawn and retire in the niglit to weave and spin and produce inncb. silk, flax linen, and cloth.

text_original: Then cloth and linen will not be sufficient.
text_normalized: Then cloth and linen will not be sufficient.

text_original: If it is asked what is it that interfered with the rulers’ attending to government and the common man’s attending to work? it must be answered, music.
text_normalized: If it is asked what is it that interfered with the rulers’ attending to government and the common man’s attending to work? it must be answered, music.

text_original: Therefore Motse said: To have music is wrong.
text_normalized: Therefore Motse said: To have music is wrong.

text_original: How do we know it is so?
text_normalized: How do we know it is so?

text_original: It is found in the Code of Punishment of T’ang " among the books of the ancient kings.
text_normalized: It is found in the Code of Punishment of T’ang " among the books of the ancient kings.

text_original: This proclaims: ‘‘ To have constant dancing in the palace is called the witch’s pleasure." As to its punishment, a gentleman will be fined six hundred and forty pieces of silk, a common man will be let go free.
text_normalized: This proclaims: ‘‘ To have constant dancing in the palace is called the witch’s pleasure." As to its punishment, a gentleman will be fined six hundred and forty pieces of silk, a common man will be let go free.

text_original: How much is the dancing.
text_normalized: How much is the dancing.

text_original: His word is all known.
text_normalized: His word is all known.

text_original: God does not bless him, therefore the nine districts are lost to him.
text_normalized: God does not bless him, therefore the nine districts are lost to him.

text_original: God does not favour him, therefore He visited him with various curses.
text_normalized: God does not favour him, therefore He visited him with various curses.

text_original: His family must he destroyed too." Now the reason that the nine districts are lost to him (Chieh of Hsia) lies in his attention to embellish music.
text_normalized: His family must he destroyed too." Now the reason that the nine districts are lost to him (Chieh of Hsia) lies in his attention to embellish music.

text_original: Quoting Wu Kuan Ch’i thereupon abandoned himself to lust and music.
text_normalized: Quoting Wu Kuan Ch’i thereupon abandoned himself to lust and music.

text_original: He drank and ate in improper places.
text_normalized: He drank and ate in improper places.

text_original: Ding ding, dong dong went the wood winds and percussion instruments in harmony.
text_normalized: Ding ding, dong dong went the wood winds and percussion instruments in harmony.

text_original: He indulged in drinldng and ate in improper places.
text_normalized: He indulged in drinldng and ate in improper places.

text_original: Brilliantly went on the dancing.
text_normalized: Brilliantly went on the dancing.

text_original: It reached the hearing of Heaven, and Heaven was not pleased." So, it was not pleasing to Heaven above and not beneficial to the people below.
text_normalized: It reached the hearing of Heaven, and Heaven was not pleased." So, it was not pleasing to Heaven above and not beneficial to the people below.

text_original: Therefore Motse said: If the gentlemen really desire to procure benefits for the world and destroy its calamities they cannot but prohibit such a thing as music.
text_normalized: Therefore Motse said: If the gentlemen really desire to procure benefits for the world and destroy its calamities they cannot but prohibit such a thing as music.
